content:
🔥 Example from Understanding Comics
McCloud uses a scene of a man talking to another man and shifts through different panel transitions to change the meaning and pacing .

📌 For Your Graphic Novel:
• Use Moment-to-Moment for suspense.
• Use Scene-to-Scene for time jumps.
• Use Aspect-to-Aspect for mood and atmosphere.

4. The Power of Iconic vs. Realistic Art Styles

McCloud introduces the spectrum of realism in comics, showing how the level of abstraction affects reader engagement .

🔥 Example: Why Peanuts Feels Personal
• Charlie Brown is highly abstract, meaning anyone can project themselves into the character.
• This is why simplified characters create a universal emotional connection.

🔥 Example: Why Maus Uses Animal Faces
• By making Jews mice and Nazis cats, Spiegelman distances the reader emotionally, allowing them to process the horror more effectively.
context: From document: 1.1.2. | Between the Frames | How Storytelling Transcends Mediums — chunk 8 (chunk 8/9)
